title: Sentence Structure Mastery Test
description: 1000 original multiple-choice sentence-structure questions for General English, VSTEP, IELTS, TOEIC, and Cambridge-style practice.
sourceNotes: Original questions generated from sentence-pattern, word-order, clause, and exam Use-of-English patterns; not copied from source materials.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: SVO word order. Context set 0001.
options: The student reviews the lesson in the library.
The student the lesson reviews in the library.
The lesson The student reviews in the library.
Reviews The student the lesson in the library.
questions.answer: The student reviews the lesson in the library.
questions.explanation: A basic English statement usually follows subject + verb + object.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: SV pattern. Context set 0002.
options: My sister arrived the report at the language centre.
My sister arrived at the language centre.
My sister was arrived at the language centre.
Arrived My sister at the language centre.
questions.answer: My sister arrived at the language centre.
questions.explanation: An intransitive verb can form a sentence with only a subject and verb.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: SVC linking verb. Context set 0003.
options: The teacher important is during the workshop.
The teacher is the lesson important.
The teacher is important during the workshop.
The teacher does important during the workshop.
questions.answer: The teacher is important during the workshop.
questions.explanation: A linking verb such as be, seem, or become is followed by a complement.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: SVOO indirect object. Context set 0004.
options: The manager gave to the teacher a new plan after the mock test.
The manager gave the teacher to a new plan after the mock test.
The manager gave a new plan the teacher after the mock test.
The manager gave the teacher a new plan after the mock test.
questions.answer: The manager gave the teacher a new plan after the mock test.
questions.explanation: Some verbs can take an indirect object before a direct object.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Direct object plus prepositional complement. Context set 0005.
options: The team sent a practice test to the team before the interview.
The team sent a practice test the team to before the interview.
The team sent the team to a practice test before the interview.
The team sent to the team a practice test before the interview.
questions.answer: The team sent a practice test to the team before the interview.
questions.explanation: Use direct object + to/for + recipient when the recipient is expressed as a prepositional phrase.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: SVOC object complement. Context set 0006.
options: The app found complete a detailed email on the course website.
The app found a detailed email was on the course website complete.
The app found a detailed email complete on the course website.
The app found that a detailed email on the course website complete.
questions.answer: The app found a detailed email complete on the course website.
questions.explanation: Object complements describe or rename the object after verbs such as make, find, or consider.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Question word order. Context set 0007.
options: Did the researcher review the lesson in the classroom?
The researcher did review the lesson in the classroom?
Did the researcher reviewed the lesson in the classroom?
Does the researcher reviewed the lesson in the classroom?
questions.answer: Did the researcher review the lesson in the classroom?
questions.explanation: Questions usually use auxiliary + subject + base verb.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Negative word order. Context set 0008.
options: The candidate does not reviews the lesson at the office.
The candidate not does review the lesson at the office.
The candidate do not review the lesson at the office.
The candidate does not review the lesson at the office.
questions.answer: The candidate does not review the lesson at the office.
questions.explanation: Use does not plus the base verb with a singular third-person subject.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Complete sentence vs fragment. Context set 0009.
options: After reviewed the lesson during the training session.
The lesson during the training session.
The company reviewed the lesson during the training session.
Because the company reviewed the lesson.
questions.answer: The company reviewed the lesson during the training session.
questions.explanation: A complete sentence needs an independent clause with a subject and a finite verb.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Compound sentence punctuation. Context set 0010.
options: The committee reviewed the lesson, and checking the details.
The committee reviewed the lesson, and the speakers checked the details.
The committee reviewed the lesson, the speakers checked the details.
The committee reviewed the lesson and checked the details the speakers.
questions.answer: The committee reviewed the lesson, and the speakers checked the details.
questions.explanation: Join two independent clauses with a comma plus a coordinating conjunction or with a semicolon.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Complex sentence. Context set 0011.
options: Because the deadline was close, the guide reviewed the lesson.
Because the deadline was close. The guide reviewed the lesson.
The guide reviewed the lesson, because.
Because the deadline was close, and reviewed the lesson.
questions.answer: Because the deadline was close, the guide reviewed the lesson.
questions.explanation: A dependent clause can be joined to an independent clause to make a complex sentence.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Relative clause placement. Context set 0012.
options: The speaker helped the candidate in the online forum who needed extra practice.
The speaker who needed extra practice helped the candidate in the online forum.
Who needed extra practice The speaker helped the candidate in the online forum.
The speaker helped the candidate who needed extra practice in the online forum.
questions.answer: The speaker helped the candidate who needed extra practice in the online forum.
questions.explanation: Place a relative clause next to the noun it describes.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Adverb placement. Context set 0013.
options: The report reviewed the lesson quickly.
The report reviewed quickly the lesson.
Quickly the lesson The report reviewed.
The report quickly the lesson reviewed.
questions.answer: The report reviewed the lesson quickly.
questions.explanation: Adverbs of manner usually come after the object or after the verb if there is no object.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Adjective order. Context set 0014.
options: The lesson chose a wooden old large desk.
The lesson chose a large old wooden desk.
The lesson chose a old wooden large desk.
The lesson chose a desk large old wooden.
questions.answer: The lesson chose a large old wooden desk.
questions.explanation: Adjectives normally follow a conventional order before the noun.

The workshop likes reading articles, taking notes, and reviewing examples.
The workshop likes articles, taking notes, and to review examples.

questions.explanation: Items in a list should use parallel grammatical forms.

It was that the lesson the designer reviewed before the team meeting.
It was the lesson that the designer reviewed before the team meeting.
questions.answer: It was the lesson that the designer reviewed before the team meeting.
questions.explanation: An it-cleft uses it + be + focused phrase + that-clause.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: There as introductory subject. Context set 0017.
options: There are several useful examples in the exam room.

Are several useful examples there in the exam room.
There is several useful examples in the exam room.
questions.answer: There are several useful examples in the exam room.
questions.explanation: Use there is or there are to introduce new information.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with correct structure. Focus: Inversion after negative adverbial. Context set 0018.

Rarely does the customer review the lesson without checking the instructions.
Rarely does the customer reviews the lesson without checking the instructions.
questions.answer: Rarely does the customer review the lesson without checking the instructions.
questions.explanation: After fronted negative adverbials, use auxiliary + subject inversion.
